design_philosophy.core_loop: Platforming and driving sequences as Kyle Reese navigating through 1984 Los Angeles and future war scenarios
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Evasion and combat against Terminator machines across mixed terrain
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Kyle Reese's perspective defending humanity against Skynet's machines
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid platformer-driving game attempting to blend film narrative pacing with action-platformer mechanics; licensed IP heavily constrains design to film locations and story beats

difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints placed far apart, requiring significant replay of sections on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty appears punitive rather than adjustable

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven level progression through film locations: 2029 future war, 1984 Los Angeles police station, nightclub
replayability_hooks: Driving level sequences offer different gameplay pacing
Multiple film locations provide environmental variety

world_building_techniques: Level design based directly on film locations
Kyle Reese as protagonist maintaining film narrative continuity
Temporal setting shifts (2029 future vs 1984 present) establish stakes

danger_signals: Terminator presence in civilian areas (1984 Los Angeles)
environmental_storytelling: Contrast between 1984 civilian infrastructure and 2029 war-torn future
Police station and nightclub as sanctuary locations

sources_used: Wikipedia - List of Terminator video games (Sega versions section)
